title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an approach to convert notes, documents, videos, and repositories into memory representations for AI agents. The technique enables agents to leverage stored information for reasoning and decision-making.
writeup: Paul Iusztin (Decoding AI) and Louis-François Bouchard (Towards AI) describe a method for converting notes, documents, videos, and code repositories into memory representations that AI agents can use. The talk frames this stored information as a substrate for agent reasoning and decision-making, drawing on memory management, knowledge distillation, agent memory, and data augmentation. The central point: heterogeneous source material can be transformed into a unified memory layer that agents query rather than treating each input type separately.
